preamble: The shift from 'bʲ' to 'ɟ' (voiced palatal stop) is the voiced counterpart to pʲ → c. This shift in place of articulation occurs when the palatalization of the bilabial stop becomes so prominent that it replaces the original labial closure.

examples.note: Rare dialectal developments where voiced palatalized labials shift to palatal stops.

sources: Ladefoged, P., & Maddieson, I. (1996). The Sounds of the World's Languages.
Schenker, A. M. (1995). The Dawn of Slavic.
